name: Imperial Ethics Credit Repair
description_short: Arizona-based credit repair company specializing in disputing negative items and credit report errors under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, with results-based pricing and attorney-backed litigation support.
description_long:
Imperial Ethics Credit Repair is a Glendale, Arizona-based credit repair company with an A+ Better Business Bureau rating and Chamber of Commerce membership. The company positions itself as a comprehensive credit solution provider that goes beyond traditional dispute services by connecting clients with real estate and mortgage professionals.

The company's core service is disputing inaccurate or unverifiable negative credit items including charge-offs, collections, bankruptcies, repossessions, student loans, and reporting errors. They operate on a results-based pricing model where clients only pay for successful removals: $17 per corrected late payment account per bureau and $77 per removal of major negative items per bureau, with a maximum lifetime cap of $470. Clients can choose between a free DIY kit or a professional service. The company also offers a free violation audit and claims to uncover reporting violations in the majority of credit reports reviewed.

What distinguishes Imperial Ethics is their partnership with attorneys to pursue litigation against creditors for Fair Credit Reporting Act violations. According to their marketing, when certain violations are found, partner attorneys pursue court cases at no additional cost to clients, with the stated goal of securing monetary damages. They also position themselves as a gateway to loan officers and realtors to help clients transition from credit repair to actual lending qualification.

While the website presents strong value propositions, some claims warrant caution. The assertion that "in the vast majority of credit reports we review, we uncover at least 1 form of reporting violation" is difficult to verify and may reflect selection bias or aggressive interpretation of reporting standards. The attorney litigation offer is contingent on finding specific violations and represents a small percentage of cases. Results in credit repair are inherently variable and dependent on individual credit situations and creditor responsiveness to disputes.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Results-based pricing model: clients only pay $17-$77 per successful removal with $470 lifetime maximum cap
Attorney-backed litigation support included at no additional cost when FCRA violations are identified
Free violation audit and free DIY credit repair kit as entry-level options
A+ Better Business Bureau rating and Chamber of Commerce membership in Arizona
Explicit referral network to mortgage lenders and realtors to support loan qualification post-repair
Uses Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 U.S.C. § 1681i) as legal foundation for disputes
Secure client portal with industry-standard encryption for document protection
cons: Claim that 'vast majority' of reviewed credit reports contain violations is unverifiable and potentially inflated; this may reflect aggressive dispute filing rather than actual creditor wrongdoing
Attorney litigation support contingent on finding specific violations—most clients will likely fall into DIY or standard dispute track, not litigation
No specific success rates, removal timelines, or outcome data published on website to substantiate effectiveness
Website content lacks detail on what constitutes a 'violation' or how disputes are documented and submitted
Marketing emphasizes emotional benefits (gateway to future, stopping past from dictating future) rather than transparent outcome metrics
best_for: Arizona residents with multiple negative items (charge-offs, collections, bankruptcies) and moderate budgets due to capped pricing
Consumers planning home or auto purchases within 6-12 months who need credit improvement before applying for loans
People who suspect creditors are violating Fair Credit Reporting Act requirements and want attorney support without upfront legal fees
diagnosis: Imperial Ethics Credit Repair is best suited for Arizona consumers with multiple negative credit items who prefer a capped-cost, results-based dispute model and want attorney backup for potential FCRA violations. The main caveat is that success depends entirely on identifying legitimate reporting errors or violations—aggressive marketing claims about finding violations in 'the vast majority' of reports should be independently verified, and most clients should expect standard dispute services rather than litigation outcomes.
services: Credit report dispute services for inaccurate or unverifiable accounts
Student loan and educational debt disputes
Identity error and unknown account corrections
Free violation audit of credit reports
Professional credit repair program with results-based fees
Attorney-backed FCRA litigation support for identified violations
Referrals to mortgage lenders and real estate professionals for loan qualification
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 6751 N Sunset Blvd Suite 320, Glendale, AZ 85305
